Quarterly review: warehouse receiving process

This report covers the receiving process at the Tulsa facility between April and June. Data comes from the warehouse management system and from interviews with six members of the receiving team.

Average dock-to-stock time fell from 31 hours in April to 19 hours in June. The improvement follows two changes made in May: the addition of a second scanning station and a revised staging layout that separates pallets by destination zone. Error rates did not change measurably over the same period and remain at 1.2 percent of received lines.

Staffing remains the main constraint. The team operated below its planned headcount for nine of the thirteen weeks in the quarter. Overtime hours rose 22 percent compared with the previous quarter, and two team members reported fatigue-related concerns in interviews. The current schedule depends on voluntary weekend shifts, which the team lead described as difficult to fill in summer months.

We recommend hiring one additional full-time receiver before the September peak, at an estimated annual cost of $52,000. The projected reduction in overtime spending covers roughly 60 percent of that cost. We also recommend a follow-up review of the staging layout in October, after peak volume, to confirm that the May changes hold under load.

Questions about this report should go to the operations team.
